title: scrophularia smithii subsp. langeana
intro: Scrophularia smithii subsp. langeana, also known as the Chinese figwort, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of China. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Scrophularia smithii subsp. langeana include its ability to relieve fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve immune function. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
